rulings.Case Title: K. MIRANDA VS. DEBBY TOMCK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Declaration Of Plaintiff Jean Claude Lair
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 19, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT DEBBY TOMEK Notice Of Motion To Strike Declaration Of Plaintiff Jean Claude Lair DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority to supports its request to strike an allegation/attachment to complaint. While moving party cites authorities regarding unlicensed practice of law, there is no argument or explanation how it has any bearing on "improper" allegations subject to striking from Complaint. Additionally, declaration contains allegations supporting K. Miranda's standing to pursue this action, a matter that is essential to UD cause of action. The Court has no authority to strike it. "While under section 436, a court at any time may, in its discretion, strike portions of a complaint that are irrelevant, improper, or not drawn in conformity with the law, matter that is essential to a cause of action should not be struck and it is error to do so." Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BROOKS VS. YNG QIAN WANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 19, 2018 line 6. DEFENDANT YING WANG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice sufficiently states severe activities, the nature of the activities; specifically threats of physical harm. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE CARTER VS. MICHAEL CLAGG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion To Quash; Memo Of P&A; Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 19, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CLAGG, MEGHAN MILLER Motion And Motion To Quash DENIED, 5 days to respond. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of notice and notice is attached to the complaint. Whether the notice is sufficient as a matter of law to terminate the tenancy or whether complaint states sufficient facts to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer is not a matter properly addressed by a motion to quash. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DELWIN M. BLACKMAN III VS. TAMARA CARLIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 19, 2018 line 8. DEFENDANT TAMARA CARLIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/REQ)
